1.title: gRPC A/B Experiment Service Handlers and Metadata Enforcement
1.summary: This cluster is the generated gRPC transport layer for experiment-related APIs, combining unary server handlers for `ABPlatform`, `AATesting`, and `Experiment` services with matching client stubs to invoke `Create`, `Update`, `Delete`, and query/selection RPCs. Each handler performs request deserialization, constructs `grpc.UnaryServerInfo`, and delegates to server methods through the optional interceptor chain. The `metadata` interceptor adds request metadata extraction into context with a required-metadata validation step, failing requests early with `InvalidArgument` when context metadata is missing.

1.title: Account/Authorization Contracts and Service Behavior Tests for Feature-Flag and Promo Rules
1.summary: This cluster spans identity and error contract models (`Role`, `Error`) plus Go rule execution/voucher preparation utilities used in promo decision workflows.

1.title: Cross-service user, feature-flag, activity, and experiment orchestration
1.summary: This cluster builds a service boundary that retrieves and normalizes user data from context/IDs/email/phone, translates gRPC responses into local DTOs, and enriches accounts with device and feature-flag state. It also integrates AB-test variant lookup to drive flag-driven behavior and includes activity handlers for creating/editing activity records plus loyalty completion checks. In parallel, it includes platform media upload handling for multipart file ingress, indicating a shared backend module coordinating user engagement, personalization, and media operations.

1.title: Campaign Promotion Orchestration and Retry Queue Operations
1.summary: This cluster appears to implement a cross-service promotional campaign flow, linking customer cart behavior (voucher validation, shipping-cost derivation, delivery-option output) with admin campaign execution paths that enqueue, retry, cancel, and reconcile campaign-related inventory discount jobs. It also provides supporting data and operational plumbing through activity-variable persistence (including Redis synchronization), campaign type metadata, bulk upload/error-reporting utilities, AB-testing attributes, and request interception to keep campaign state, targeting, and fault handling coordinated.

1.title: AI Assistant Phase Execution and HTTP Provider Gateway Pipeline
1.summary: This cluster defines the core AI chat orchestration flow, combining planning-execution logic with terminal escalation handling for unrecoverable errors in the cortex assistant phases. It also includes an AI gateway stack that constructs provider runtime/client configuration, normalizes and preprocesses requests, forwards HTTP calls, resolves provider metadata, and enforces read/timeout/validation behavior for safe request handling. The included Java helper method `InventoryDiscountService.copy` is a low-level stream-copy utility for transferring bytes between input and output streams.

1.title: Activity Repository Testing Surface and Messaging/Utility Helpers
1.summary: This cluster covers the `activity.repository` layer in the loyalty backend, including test scaffolding (`NewMock`) and a broad set of repository behaviors for creating, editing, and querying activity/customer records (coins, multipliers, validity windows, IDs, and notification/device lookups). It also includes `RandomUtil.generateRandomString`, a helper that constructs alphanumeric random tokens from random bytes for identifier-like values. Separately, `pubsub.Publish` is a Go publisher utility that sends messages with payload, attributes, and ordering key to Google Pub/Sub topics with debug logging.

1.title: Cross-API Contract and Client-Service Layer for Accurate Order Operations
1.summary: This cluster bundles API design artifacts spanning HTTP and service-contract layers, including a FastAPI REST template and Java domain models/enums used for voucher and dynamic landing-page sizing payloads. It also includes Accurate service symbols (e.g., `GetSalesInvoice`, `SalesOrderSave`, `VendorSave`, `Webhook`, plus test-oriented service entry points) that describe sales/invoice/vendor/order webhook workflows backed by protobuf definitions. Together, these pieces define a shared integration pattern for building and exercising external-facing APIs with standardized payload structures, method naming, and validation behavior.

1.title: Marketing and Attribution Service API/Test Cluster for Activity Rules, Accounts, and External Integrations
1.summary: This cluster groups HTTP-layer handler methods for activity-rule and activity-variable management (create, read, update, delete) plus shared response/context helpers used by service endpoints. It also contains unit tests and mock repositories for account detail retrieval, ad/asset scanning and inventory APIs, and attribution deep-link generation/editing, covering both happy paths and failure/edge cases. Overall, it focuses on API contract and external service interaction validation across related business domains.

1.title: gRPC Service Boundary and Unit-Test Cluster for Accounts, AMS, Alert, and Anti-Abuse Modules
1.summary: This cluster covers gRPC service wiring and test scaffolding across multiple Go modules, including generated client/server helpers, mock constructors, and a unary metadata interceptor used for cross-service calls. It also includes unit tests and helper constructors for core service flows such as customer/admin lookups, purchase/order creation, asset inventory status updates, alert sender retrieval, and blacklist email repository/service operations. Together, these symbols indicate a service-integration layer focused on validating external service interfaces and repository behaviors with mocked dependencies rather than runtime business logic itself.

1.title: Cart and Campaign Pricing Orchestration for Checkout
1.summary: This cluster centers on cart pricing and checkout-domain modeling, defining item and price-component types (including loyalty, PWP, discounts) plus cart utilities for weight adjustments, grouped price totals, and loyalty processing. It also wires in campaign/experiment and account-driven enrichment paths (customer lookups, campaign discounts, variant IDs, order request flags) used during order creation, supported by service-layer infrastructure such as auth clients, request interception, and resource/lifecycle helpers.

1.title: ClickHouse Campaign & Keyword Analytics List Service Pipeline
1.summary: This cluster builds and executes ClickHouse-backed list queries for campaign and keyword reporting, including request normalization, sorting, filtering, and cursor handling for paginated handlers. It maps rows from ClickHouse into API response objects, assembling campaign/keyword items with budgets, status, and derived metrics via shared metric conversion helpers and value extraction utilities. Supporting index builders and request/response helpers tie together service-layer criteria, query execution, and formatted output for both campaign and keyword endpoints.

1.title: Inventory Stock Transfer and Bulk Upload Safety Cohesion
1.summary: This cluster gathers domain helpers and service logic for inventory stock movement workflows, including unique-ID extraction, deduplication, and aggregate stock calculations that support transfer, adjustment, and schedule validation. It also defines transfer request predicates (e.g., rack, expiry-date, and transfer-all changes), status-role checks, and an in-transit rollback publisher used when stock adjustments fail. The accompanying tests exercise schedule comparison, SKU/asset limits, access-role rules, and positive/negative stock-trail creation scenarios for bulk upload/transfer operations.

1.title: Product Catalog Intake, Registration, and Messaging Cluster
1.summary: This cluster centers on WIMS product catalog domain logic for bulk updates and registration workflows, including bulk MSL row decoding/error tracking, product/category/packaging/rack/location lookup helpers, UOM classification and defaults, and computed registration business values (such as proposed margin). It also includes service-level hooks for seeding categories and emitting AIM full-update messages, while the included `astro-proto` setup indicates protobuf/gRPC message contracts are packaged and shared for integration across services.

1.title: Purchase-Order Feature Gating and Inventory Replenishment Data Access Utilities
1.summary: This cluster centers on two responsibilities: purchase-order feature control via `isManageExp` and a broad set of DAO/model helpers that fetch, de-duplicate, and aggregate inventory, movement, and product data by ID-based keys. The DAO functions build maps and sets from related entities such as racks, tasks, status notes, and purchase-inbound relationships to support replenishment and inbound workflow calculations. Together they form a data-composition layer for replenishment execution and PO management, including product/image/category enrichment for downstream business logic.

1.title: Supply Order Stock Adjustment, Validation, and Rollback Coordination
1.summary: This cluster wires together supply-order, inventory, and movement DAO methods that aggregate stock and quantity context (including rack-excluded availability, in-transit quantities, and mapped order/product/inventory relationships) used to decide and execute stock operations. It includes domain predicates and mappers for order state classification (direct, return, bad-stock), replenishment progress, and package/location lookups, plus stock-history/config translation to keep stock mutations consistent across workflows. It also covers rollback and integration paths by handling stock adjustment response parsing, emitting centralized IMS rollback events, and providing mock configuration access for supply-order constraints.

1.title: Request Context, I/O Transport, and ID/Status Mapping Utilities
1.summary: This cluster groups cross-cutting infrastructure helpers for moving data between system boundaries: HTTP request interceptors that propagate header metadata into request context, HTTP client wrappers that standardize outbound calls and JSON handling, and file helpers for upload/download and header injection. The DAO methods complement that boundary layer by extracting distinct order/item IDs and building status-note keyed lookup maps, enabling efficient reconciliation and filtering of purchase and supply order detail records.

1.title: Background Processing and Area Lookup Infrastructure
1.summary: This cluster combines three support concerns: protobuf service contracts for ERP job retry and WMS task operations, a small version-parsing utility, and a richer runtime layer for area data retrieval and background process lifecycle management. The dominant behavior appears to be infrastructure-oriented code that resolves area records for application use while coordinating long-running or periodic worker processes with startup, tracking, shutdown, and benchmark support.

1.title: Cross-Service Commerce Domain Models and Knowledge Automation Utilities
1.summary: This cluster mixes two concerns: lightweight automation scripts for maintaining internal knowledge artifacts and documentation, and a broad set of domain/entity definitions spread across Astro microservices. The service files model core commerce and operations concepts such as chat context, products, orders, inventory, promotions, loyalty, scheduling, observability, and external integrations, suggesting a shared backend platform where many bounded contexts exchange structured data while tooling keeps supporting docs, indexes, and sample outputs up to date.

1.title: Ad Product Enrichment And Flexi Campaign Pricing Bridge
1.summary: This cluster ties ad-scanning workflows to product-rich campaign data, with `admanager.adManagerService.ScanAds`, `ScanCampaigns`, `mapCampaignAd`, and `mapScanAdItem` pulling ad records and normalizing embedded product metadata such as variants, ROAS multipliers, and product status. It also includes the protobuf accessors that expose cart, pricing, discount, and product attributes consumed by `flexi_campaign.Handler.CalculateFlexiCampaign`, making this cluster a boundary between ad/catalog retrieval and downstream campaign pricing calculations.

1.title: Authenticated Service Integration and Validation Helpers
1.summary: This cluster combines low-level protobuf field accessors with service-layer glue used to build authenticated, cross-service workflows. The substantive logic centers on extracting the current user from request context, validating tokens and auth clients, shaping campaign payloads based on auth state, and performing customer or device fraud checks, while the many `Get*` methods are generated request/response accessors that support those flows.

1.title: Inventory Stock Adjustment, Transfer, and Rollback Logging
1.summary: This cluster handles inventory stock movements and manual adjustments across product details, including transfers, quantity and reserved-quantity changes, and special handling for virtual versus non-virtual items. It enriches adjustment requests, generates audit logs and rollback payloads, hashes requests for deduplication, and supports duplicate-alert and bulk-processing flows around stock corrections.

1.title: ERP Vendor Management API
1.summary: This cluster provides methods for managing vendor submissions, contacts, locations, and documents within an ERP system.

1.title: Bulk Product Addition and Management
1.summary: This cluster handles bulk product addition, cancellation, campaign management, and user deletion operations.

1.title: User Account Protobuf Message Descriptors
1.summary: This cluster consists of generated protobuf `Descriptor()` methods for request and response message types that define the user account service contract. The messages cover core identity and access flows such as authentication, activation/deactivation, password recovery, OTP/TOTP generation, PIN creation, user creation, and email-based user lookup.

1.title: Campaign, Repository, and gRPC Bootstrapping Cluster for Promo Platform
1.summary: This cluster groups factory constructors for domain repositories/services across campaign, attribution, AB testing, referrals, customer data, fraud, Google BigQuery, Google Cloud, and related adapters, standardizing dependency injection across the promo backend. It also includes gRPC application startup and Pub/Sub wiring (`InitApps`, `NewInfrastructure`, `Run`, pubsub register/listen, config builders) to run the campaign/queue processing runtime. The `flexi_campaign` helpers and benchmark setup/teardown functions provide Redis/DB-backed test data orchestration for measuring campaign usage behavior.

1.title: AdManager Campaign Descriptor Methods
1.summary: This cluster contains descriptor methods for campaign-related entities, including campaign, campaign ad, keyword, and product ad descriptors, which are used for protocol buffer serialization.

1.title: Campaigns GWP Protocol
1.summary: This cluster defines methods for managing campaign operations, including creating, editing, deleting, and checking eligibility for campaigns with multi-sku support.

1.title: Replenishment Task Management
1.summary: This cluster manages tasks related to replenishment, including task cancellation, completion, configuration, scheduling, and eligibility checks.

1.title: Buyer Order Protobuf Descriptor Layer
1.summary: This cluster is generated protobuf metadata for the buyer-side order domain, specifically the `Descriptor()` methods for request and response message types. The messages cover order calculation, edit flows, pricing, timing, promos, SLA, branding, cancellation, and child-order payloads, indicating this layer defines the schema surface used by buyer order APIs rather than business logic.

1.title: Loyalty System API Endpoints
1.summary: This cluster contains methods for managing loyalty points, coin transactions, orders, and customer data, providing interfaces for adding coins, canceling orders, checking eligibility, and converting points.

1.title: GWP Protocol Methods
1.summary: This cluster contains methods for managing GWP (Gift Wrap Protocol) operations, including creating, editing, deleting, and checking eligibility of gift wrap items.

1.title: Commercial Reorder List and Request Changes Protobuf Descriptors
1.summary: This cluster is generated protobuf descriptor code for the commercial reorder list domain, covering adjustment values, audit logs, reorder list confirmation and deletion flows, and request-changes operations. The methods expose message metadata for serialization and reflection rather than implementing business logic, indicating this cluster defines the API schema surface for these commercial workflows.

1.title: AMS Asset Management Protobuf Descriptor Layer
1.summary: This cluster is generated protobuf reflection code for the AMS asset-management domain, covering core asset entities, categories, inventory, statuses, images, and related purchase or supply order request and response messages. These `Descriptor()` methods do not implement business logic; they expose protobuf schema metadata so the runtime can serialize, deserialize, and introspect the API contract for asset and inventory operations.

1.title: Fraud Detection and Risk Assessment
1.summary: This cluster handles fraud detection and risk assessment operations, including order approval, customer fraud checks, and fraud score calculations.

1.title: Route Wave Location and Destination Management
1.summary: This cluster handles the lifecycle of route-wave definitions, including creating and updating route waves, transforming requests into DAO models, and validating destination uniqueness, sequencing, and maximum destination limits. It also supports bulk CSV upload flows by grouping records, extracting referenced location IDs, parsing add/edit fields, and validating upload actions, while the location controller provides paginated location data used by these workflows.

1.title: Milestone Protocol
1.summary: This cluster defines methods for managing milestones, including dynamic voucher time, active milestone retrieval, internal milestone lookup, and progress tracking.

1.title: Generated Protobuf Descriptors for Picking Completion and Detail APIs
1.summary: This cluster consists of autogenerated `Descriptor()` methods for protobuf message types in the fulfillment `picking` domain. It defines schema metadata for requests, responses, nested validation/data payloads, and batch/SLOC variants covering picking completion, scan completion, self-picking, and retrieval of picking details.

1.title: Hub Staff Protobuf Message Descriptors
1.summary: This cluster is generated Go protobuf reflection code for the `hub_staff` API schema, covering request and response message types for staff lifecycle, role changes, availability, location, and export workflows. Each `Descriptor()` method exposes metadata for a specific message type so protobuf tooling, marshaling, and RPC infrastructure can identify and work with the underlying schema definitions.

1.title: Admin Order Protobuf Message Descriptors
1.summary: This cluster is generated Protocol Buffers reflection code for the admin order domain, covering request, response, filter, fulfillment, delivery, SLA, and return-order message types. The `Descriptor` methods expose protobuf metadata for admin-facing order APIs, enabling runtime introspection, serialization support, and message registration rather than implementing business logic itself.

1.title: Generated Auth Protobuf Message Descriptors
1.summary: This cluster consists of generated `Descriptor()` methods for authentication and token-related protobuf message types in the `auth` package. The messages cover admin, customer, vendor, MQTT, Intools, and Zendesk flows for authentication, authorization, and temporary or exchange token generation.

1.title: Finops Protobuf Descriptor Methods
1.summary: This cluster contains descriptor methods for Finops proto files, defining the structure and message formats for financial operations data.

1.title: Loyalty Redeem Reward Protobuf Message Descriptors
1.summary: This cluster is the generated protobuf reflection layer for the loyalty redeem-reward domain, exposing `Descriptor()` metadata for request, response, and nested data message types. The covered messages span core redeem-reward operations such as activation by ID, reward image attachment, bulk reward insertion, quota checking, deletion, and retrieval of available rewards, indicating an API contract module rather than executable business logic.

1.title: ERP Vendor Protobuf Message Descriptors
1.summary: This cluster is generated Protobuf metadata for the ERP vendor domain, covering message types used for vendor creation, submission, lookup, contacts, banking, locations, and document download workflows. The `Descriptor` methods expose schema information for nested request and response objects, indicating this code supports serialization and RPC contract definitions rather than business logic.

1.title: Supply Order Loading Protocol
1.summary: This cluster defines methods for handling supply order loading operations, including cancelation, retrieval, and driver-related interactions.

1.title: Notification Template and Email Sender Protobuf Metadata
1.summary: This cluster consists of generated protobuf `Descriptor()` methods for notification-domain message types related to alert templates, email templates, push notification templates, WhatsApp templates, and email sender configuration. Architecturally, it provides schema reflection metadata for request, response, and nested data payload types used by create/delete notification management APIs rather than implementing business logic itself.

1.title: Generated PWP Promo Protobuf Message Descriptors
1.summary: This cluster consists of auto-generated `Descriptor()` methods for Protocol Buffers message types in the `pwp` promo domain. It provides runtime schema metadata for PWP campaign management, bulk product operations, cancellation flows, rule lookups, and related request/response payloads used by serialization and reflection machinery.

1.title: Repository Maintenance and Routing Validation Tooling
1.summary: This cluster groups internal developer utilities rather than product runtime logic: one script restructures a technical requirements document, another probes and self-tests subagent routing decisions, and a third synchronizes Markdown and prompt assets into Codex skill formats. The included tests focus on enforcing priority-to-route mappings, so the overall cluster acts as operational tooling for content maintenance, configuration synchronization, and routing verification.

1.title: Refund Protobuf Message Descriptors
1.summary: This cluster is generated protobuf reflection code for the refund domain, exposing `Descriptor()` methods for request, response, data, error, and balance-related message types. Together, these types define the wire-level schema for refund workflows such as eligibility checks, refund creation, compensation calculation, complaint reasons, refund detail/list retrieval, and point-balance operations.

1.title: A/B Testing Experiment Protobuf API Models
1.summary: This cluster represents generated protobuf descriptor methods for the A/B testing domain, covering experiment definitions, experiment target channels, testing users, and experiment types. It also includes request and response message schemas for creating, deleting, listing, and querying active experiment variants and attributes, indicating a service contract for managing and resolving active experiments across channels.

1.title: Protobuf Enum Stringification Helpers
1.summary: This cluster consists of generated `String()` methods for protobuf enum types spread across ads, campaigns, approval, discovery, chat, capability, casbin, and commerce-related packages. Each method delegates to `protoimpl.X.EnumStringOf` with the enum descriptor and numeric value, providing a consistent way to convert enum constants into their protobuf-defined string names for serialization, logging, and debugging.

1.title: Forecast Data Management
1.summary: This cluster manages various forecast data operations, including exporting CSV files, retrieving distribution and trend data, and handling product summaries.

1.title: GWP Promotion Protobuf Message Descriptors
1.summary: This cluster is generated Protobuf reflection code for the GWP (Gift With Purchase) promotion domain, covering request and response messages for campaign lifecycle operations such as create, edit, cancel, eligibility checks, status changes, product retrieval, and user usage management. The `Descriptor()` methods expose message schema metadata used by the Protobuf runtime, indicating this cluster defines the serialized API contract rather than implementing business logic itself.

1.title: GWP Promo Protobuf Message Descriptors
1.summary: This cluster is generated Go protobuf metadata for the Gift With Purchase (GWP) promotion domain, covering request, response, and entity message types such as eligibility checks, campaign management, product retrieval, status changes, and user usage tracking. The `Descriptor` methods expose schema descriptors for reflection and serialization rather than implementing business logic, indicating this cluster defines the wire-contract layer for GWP-related APIs.

1.title: Generated Protobuf Descriptors for Notification Push APIs
1.summary: This cluster consists of auto-generated `Descriptor()` methods for protobuf message types in the notification domain, all tied to the `push_notification.proto` schema. The messages cover push-token deletion, bulk notification deletion, customer notification retrieval and settings, unread-status queries, device notification payloads, and CleverTap webhook user-insert request/response structures.

1.title: Loyalty Service Protobuf Message Descriptors
1.summary: This cluster is generated Protocol Buffers metadata for the loyalty domain, covering request and response message types for coin accrual, coin reversal, point-to-coin conversion, redeem eligibility checks, banner retrieval, and coin transaction listing. The `Descriptor` methods expose protobuf schema information for these messages and nested types, enabling serialization, reflection, and RPC integration rather than implementing business logic directly.

1.title: Replenishment Workflow Protobuf Request/Response Descriptors
1.summary: This cluster is generated protobuf metadata for the warehouse replenishment domain, covering task execution, putaway and letdown handling, rack eligibility lookup, configuration management, and schedule CRUD operations. The `Descriptor` methods do not implement business logic themselves; they expose schema descriptors for the corresponding replenishment request and response message types so the RPC and serialization layers can reflect over them consistently.

1.title: Fraud Protobuf Message Descriptor Layer
1.summary: This cluster is generated protobuf metadata for the fraud domain’s request and response message types, covering order approval/rejection, fraud scoring, customer fraud lookup, fraud shield checks, and shield-eligibility queries. The `Descriptor` methods expose schema descriptors for serialization, reflection, and RPC/message handling rather than implementing business logic themselves.

1.title: Generated Order Admin Protobuf Descriptors
1.summary: This cluster is generated Go code for Protocol Buffers message metadata in the order domain, specifically the `Descriptor()` methods for admin, driver-management, order-detail, activity, and SLA-related request/response types. It supports protobuf reflection and serialization for a broad set of order service contracts rather than implementing business logic directly.

1.title: Milestone Protobuf Descriptor Accessors
1.summary: This cluster consists of generated Protocol Buffers `Descriptor()` methods for milestone-related request, response, and nested message types. Together, they provide runtime schema metadata for milestone APIs covering active milestone state, landing page content, participation, progress, pinning, and internal milestone lookup/list operations.

1.title: WMS Supply Order Inbound Protobuf Message Descriptors
1.summary: This cluster consists of generated protobuf `Descriptor()` methods for Warehouse Management System inbound supply-order messages. The messages model inbound receiving workflows such as scanned product detail and deletion, inbound item and quantity validation, multi-SKU item detail lookup, koli-code product mapping, and putaway task listing/reset operations.

1.title: Fraud Rule Management
1.summary: This cluster handles CRUD operations for fraud rules, including creating, deleting, updating, and finding rules, with support for various rule versions.

1.title: Flexi Campaign Handler Validation and Error-Path Tests
1.summary: This cluster is a coverage-focused test suite for the gRPC `flexi_campaign` handler layer, exercising request validation, service interaction, and response shaping across flexi campaign operations. The tests concentrate on invalid or zero user IDs, empty results, optional filter handling, duplicate product IDs, and propagation of promo-service or generic service errors for calculate, create, detail, unified, list, and usage endpoints.

1.title: Generated Order Protobuf Message Descriptors
1.summary: This cluster consists of auto-generated `Descriptor()` methods for the order domain’s Protocol Buffers message types, covering carts, customers, orders, fulfillment, status, and request/response DTOs. Each method exposes protobuf schema metadata for reflection and serialization, indicating this cluster is infrastructure-level generated code rather than business logic.

1.title: Location Geometry Service Methods
1.summary: This cluster contains methods for handling location geometry operations such as bulk deletion, coordinate retrieval, and isochrone calculations.

1.title: WMS Supply Order Loading Protobuf Message Descriptors
1.summary: This cluster is generated protobuf reflection code for the WMS supply order loading domain, covering request and response messages for grouping, cancellation, detail retrieval, driver/loader lookup, lost koli tracking, and staging/provider configuration. These `Descriptor()` methods do not implement business logic themselves; they expose schema metadata so the loading service’s messages can be serialized, registered, and introspected consistently across RPC boundaries.

1.title: Loyalty Membership Protobuf Schema Descriptors
1.summary: This cluster is generated protobuf descriptor plumbing for the loyalty membership domain, covering request/response messages and data models for membership state, status, landing pages, popup content, purchase benefit history, and payment-linked membership lookup. Together, these `Descriptor()` methods expose protobuf metadata for serialization, reflection, and RPC/message binding rather than implementing business logic themselves.

1.title: Casbin Policy Management
1.summary: This cluster handles policy management operations such as rule creation, deletion, checking policies, and managing role-based access control using the Casbin library.

1.title: Response Descriptor Methods
1.summary: This cluster contains methods for describing response structures in a protocol buffer format, including pagination, file downloads, image responses, and product cards.

1.title: Generated Order Driver Protobuf Descriptors
1.summary: This cluster consists of autogenerated `Descriptor()` methods for protobuf request, response, and domain message types in the order/driver API. Together they expose protobuf schema metadata for order availability, selection, tracking, navigation, pagination, tips, and related delivery structures so the runtime can perform reflection, serialization, and compatibility handling.

1.title: Protobuf Descriptor Accessors for Discovery Banner Messages
1.summary: This cluster consists of generated `Descriptor()` methods for the `discovery` banner-related protobuf message types, covering buyer, admin, image, segmentation, category, upload, and template download request/response models. Each method exposes protobuf metadata by returning the shared compressed file descriptor plus the message’s type index, enabling reflection, serialization tooling, and runtime schema introspection rather than implementing application logic.

1.title: WMS Purchase Order Protobuf Schema Metadata
1.summary: This cluster is generated protobuf descriptor code for the WMS purchase order domain, covering request and response message types for purchase order creation, validation, fulfillment, detail retrieval, scheduling, GRN data, and vendor or destination-based queries. Its role is reflective rather than behavioral: each `Descriptor()` method exposes message schema metadata so the broader system can serialize, deserialize, and inspect purchase-order-related API contracts consistently.

1.title: Data Migration Backfills for Product Packaging and Purchase Orders
1.summary: This cluster implements and tests data backfill workflows that repair or enrich legacy operational data, especially product packaging structures, base UOM/barcode relationships, product attributes, cloud kitchen schedules, and purchase orders. It loads legacy records, maps them into new domain rows, enforces base/primary packaging rules, updates product fields only when safe, persists the transformed data in transactions, and emits notifications around backfill execution.

1.title: Commercial Insight and Reporting Protobuf Models
1.summary: This cluster is generated protobuf descriptor code for the commercial insight and reporting API schema, covering request and response messages for fulfillment, OTIF, on-time ratio, brand performance, product performance, and report download workflows. It defines the message metadata for chart datasets, overview payloads, and downloaded file/event envelopes so these analytics and reporting contracts can be serialized, reflected on, and consumed consistently across services.

1.title: MCP Shopping Search and Recommendation Orchestration
1.summary: This cluster implements a local MCP server layer that parses tool-call parameters and dispatches shopping actions such as product search, cart updates, and recommendation retrieval. Beneath that interface, the `SearchService` loads product data, ranks and filters results for relevance, supports shopping-list fallback matching, and exposes product/cart operations, with tests covering search quality, recommendations, suggestions, and product detail behavior.

1.title: Purchase Order Management
1.summary: This cluster handles operations related to creating, retrieving, and managing purchase orders, including creating documents, canceling orders, and fetching related schedules and attributes.

1.title: Generated Protobuf Descriptor Layer for Order Pub/Sub Messages
1.summary: This cluster is the reflection and schema-registration surface for the `CommonMessage` protobuf and its nested order, delivery, fulfillment, inventory, and miscellaneous submessages used in pub/sub flows. Each `Descriptor()` method is generated code that exposes compiled message metadata so protobuf tooling can serialize, inspect, and route a shared event envelope across many order-domain payload variants.

1.title: Hub Staff Protobuf Schema Initialization
1.summary: This cluster is generated Go protobuf bootstrap code for the `hub_staff` domain, responsible for registering file descriptors, message types, oneof wrappers, and service metadata for staff-related APIs. Collectively, it wires together interdependent schemas spanning attendance, scheduling, payroll, budgeting, staffing, and vendor data so the package is fully initialized at program startup.

1.title: Packing Admin Protobuf Message Descriptors
1.summary: This cluster is the generated protobuf reflection layer for the packing admin domain, exposing `Descriptor()` metadata for request, response, and data messages used by packing workflows. The messages cover packer assignment, staging, packing completion, partial fulfillment approval, removable items, and rich order/inventory payloads, indicating this cluster defines the schema contract for packing-related admin APIs rather than business logic.

1.title: Page Management API
1.summary: This cluster manages page creation, deletion, retrieval, and pagination operations, providing endpoints for interacting with page data.

1.title: Loyalty Earn-Coin Protobuf Message Descriptors
1.summary: This cluster is generated protobuf metadata for the loyalty earn-coin domain, covering request and response message types for activating, deleting, listing, and validating earn-coin configurations. The messages model admin and lookup workflows around coin multipliers, candidate products, product/location targeting, and channel/location-specific coin rules, with each `Descriptor()` method exposing protobuf schema information for reflection and serialization.

1.title: Generated Protobuf Chat Schema Descriptors
1.summary: This cluster is the generated Protocol Buffers reflection layer for the chat API’s request, response, session, message, attachment, tool-call, and suggested-prompt message types. Each `Descriptor` method exposes the encoded schema metadata for its message so protobuf runtime code can perform serialization, deserialization, introspection, and RPC/message handling consistently across the chat service.

1.title: Loyalty Event Protobuf Message Descriptors
1.summary: This cluster is generated protobuf reflection code for the `loyalty` event domain, exposing `Descriptor()` metadata for request, response, and nested payload types. The messages cover event CRUD operations plus order-detail editing, order-event persistence/retrieval, and event-email sending, indicating a service contract layer for loyalty event workflows rather than handwritten business logic.

1.title: Seller Order Detail Interface
1.summary: This cluster defines methods for handling order details related to seller orders, including retrieval and descriptor information.

1.title: Recommendation Protobuf Message Descriptors
1.summary: This cluster is generated protobuf descriptor code for the recommendation domain, defining the schema metadata for recommendation request, response, enum, and data messages. It covers multiple recommendation features including product affinity, keyword affinity, category ranking, buyer cross-sell, contextual cross-sell, favorites, reorder suggestions, and import state transport models used by RPC/serialization layers.

1.title: Protobuf Reflection Descriptors for Order System Dev Messages
1.summary: This cluster is Go code generated from `order/system/dev.proto` that implements `Descriptor()` on many request/response message types for the protobuf runtime. Each method returns the gzipped raw file descriptor plus the message’s index path, enabling protobuf reflection, type registration, and correct (de)serialization for the order-system “dev” API surface.

1.title: Fraud Rule Management Protobuf Message Descriptors
1.summary: This cluster is generated Go protobuf glue for the Fraud “rule” domain, exposing `Descriptor()` metadata for request/response and entity messages like `Rule`, `RuleV3`, and `GroupRule`. It supports CRUD-style operations (create, delete, find by id, list all) plus a V3 API surface and bulk priority updates, enabling protobuf reflection/serialization for RPC and storage boundaries.

1.title: Loyalty Campaign & Discount Protobuf Message Descriptors
1.summary: This cluster is the generated Protobuf reflection/descriptor plumbing for the Loyalty “campaign” domain, exposing `Descriptor()` metadata for campaign and campaign-discount request/response message types. It underpins serialization, runtime type introspection, and tooling for operations like create/delete/find campaigns and discounts, bulk discount upload, querying candidate redeem-reward discounts, and retrieving campaign type.

1.title: Product Registration System
1.summary: This cluster handles the registration of products with various additional information, assets, and pricing details.

1.title: User Vendor Operations
1.summary: This cluster handles operations related to creating, retrieving, and managing user-vendor relationships, including bulk creation, pagination, and email resending.

1.title: Loyalty System API Boundary
1.summary: This cluster defines the API methods for managing loyalty data, including creating, editing, deleting, and fetching loyalty records and widgets.

1.title: Casbin Authorization Protobuf Message Descriptors
1.summary: This cluster is generated Go protobuf descriptor code for Casbin-based authorization data structures, including `CasbinRule`, related enums (`Domain`, `Effect`), and request/response messages. It supports operations like checking the Casbin model/policy data and creating/deleting Casbin rules (including bulk role-rule operations) by exposing protobuf reflection metadata via `Descriptor()` methods.

1.title: Order Alternative Management
1.summary: This cluster handles operations related to order alternatives, including confirming packing, fulfilling locations, and managing line item alternatives.

1.title: Cart Service Methods for Adding and Managing Cart Items
1.summary: This cluster contains methods for adding cart items, managing loyalty and reward carts, and handling inventory and quantity constraints in the cart service.

1.title: Product BOM CRUD, Mapping, and Listing
1.summary: This cluster implements creation, update, and retrieval of Product Bills of Materials (BOMs) with their component lines, including paginated listing and “with components” fetches by ID(s). It provides DTO helpers to collect referenced product IDs, convert requests into DAO/update payloads (including stock recalculation triggers), validate component product types, and service-layer utilities for transactional repositories and efficient product/component mapping for joined query results.

1.title: ERP Order Processing and Response Methods
1.summary: This cluster contains methods for handling order-related responses in an ERP system, including order details, cost calculations, delivery origins, and product data.

1.title: User Role Management API
1.summary: This cluster handles bulk and individual operations for creating, deleting, and retrieving user-vendor role associations, providing methods for managing user roles in a distributed system.

1.title: Brand Submission Protobuf API Message Descriptors
1.summary: This cluster is the Go protobuf-generated `Descriptor()` implementations for Brand Submission domain messages, exposing each message’s metadata and wiring it to the compiled `.proto` file descriptor. It covers request/response shapes for operations like fetching submissions by ID, listing submissions, deleting submissions, and retrieving related reference data such as buyer targets, campaign objectives/targets, packages, and promo criteria.

1.title: Product DTO Mapping and Override Pipeline
1.summary: This cluster converts incoming product payloads (gRPC/Protobuf and bulk-upload requests) into internal create/update request structures, assembling categories, images, translations, and attribute details/options. It also applies a layered set of product “override” rules (flags, attributes, documents, expiration/period dates, shelf/packaging, tax/pricing) to produce final product data used by create/update flows, with tests covering the CreateProduct gRPC handler paths.

1.title: User Authentication and User Management Interface
1.summary: This cluster handles user authentication, login, user creation, OTP generation, and user detail retrieval, providing methods for both business operations and data retrieval.

1.title: Purchase Request Management
1.summary: This cluster handles operations related to creating, deleting, retrieving, and confirming purchase requests, including detailed and summary data interactions.

1.title: Loyalty Rules Protobuf Message Descriptors
1.summary: This cluster consists of generated protobuf `Descriptor()` methods for loyalty-rules messages used by the loyalty domain API. It defines reflection metadata for requests/responses covering order-completion checks and CRUD-style operations on loyalty activities (create, edit variants, detail, delete), enabling serialization, type introspection, and tooling support in Go.

1.title: Protobuf Message Descriptors for Page CRUD and Permissions APIs
1.summary: This cluster is generated Go protobuf code that exposes reflection metadata (`Descriptor()` methods) for the `page` API message types. It supports serialization and runtime introspection for operations like creating/deleting pages, fetching pages (including JSON forms), listing pages with pagination, and managing page permissions and main-menu page retrieval by user ID.

1.title: Purchase Order Completion (Receiving → Inventory) Test Cluster
1.summary: This cluster tests the `UpdatePurchaseOrderComplete` service workflow that finalizes a receiving purchase order by locking/idempotency-guarding the operation, starting a DB transaction, loading PO/items/user/product data, and computing total actual quantities. It validates that completion updates item actual quantities and inventory IDs, performs stock adjustment and optional rack transfers, creates/updates related receiving artifacts (e.g., GRN/FPO), sets the correct final PO status (completed or partially fulfilled), and commits/rolls back with proper error handling and lock cleanup.

1.title: Hub Staff Scheduling Protobuf Message Descriptors
1.summary: This cluster consists of protobuf-generated `Descriptor()` methods for `hub_staff` scheduling-related request/response and data messages (create/change/delete/list/import/export schedules, staff details, approvals, templates, and emergency locations). These descriptors provide the metadata needed for protobuf reflection, serialization/deserialization, and gRPC/tooling integration around the Hub Staff scheduling domain.

1.title: Bulk Upload Protobuf API Definitions (Go)
1.summary: This cluster is protoc-generated Go code that exposes protobuf descriptors for the “bulk upload” domain, covering services, service features (including templates and permissions), and bulk upload transactions. It defines the request/response message types for creating services/features/permissions, listing and fetching services/features/transactions, and downloading transaction files (including V2 variants).

1.title: Fulfillment Order Line Item Protobuf Descriptors (Go)
1.summary: This cluster is generated Go protobuf code that provides reflection descriptors for the `order_line_item` message types used in the fulfillment domain (requests/responses/entities like line items, item types, labels, packing details, and various “get/insert/count/change” operations). Each `Descriptor()` method returns the gzipped raw `.proto` descriptor blob plus the message’s index, enabling protobuf runtime introspection and serialization plumbing.

1.title: Customer Order API Protobuf Descriptors
1.summary: This cluster is generated Protobuf descriptor code for the `order` customer-facing API, defining the serialized request/response message shapes used over gRPC. It covers customer order creation and retrieval (list/detail/ongoing), along with related payloads such as address, driver/ETA, payment/fees, vouchers/tips/reviews/tickets, plus operational messages for SLA/fulfillment checks and inserting orders into an order pool.

1.title: Product Registration Protobuf-to-DTO Mapping Layer
1.summary: This cluster converts an incoming `wims.ProductRegistration` protobuf message into the service’s internal `ProductRegistration` DTO, handling nil-safety and pointer normalization. It decomposes the registration into a set of focused mappers for nested domains (general data, additional info, handling, grammation variants, pricing/final pricing, assortment/planning, inventory/outbound details, assets/images, locations/clusters, categorization/branding, business tagging, substitutions, and failure metadata).

1.title: WMS Protobuf Descriptor Initialization Cluster
1.summary: This cluster is generated Go protobuf bootstrap code for the `wms` package, responsible for constructing and registering file descriptors, message types, and related metadata at startup. It wires inter-proto dependencies (e.g., catalog depending on location) and sets up safe-mode exporters when `protoimpl.UnsafeEnabled` is false, covering WMS domains like catalog, fresh purchase (inbound/packing), locations, orders, putaway tasks, racks, and related entities.

1.title: Go Protobuf Message Descriptor Boilerplate (location/common.pb.go)
1.summary: This cluster is protoc-generated Go code that provides `Descriptor()` methods for multiple `location` protobuf message types. Each method returns the gzipped file descriptor bytes plus the message’s index path, enabling protobuf reflection, type metadata lookup, and compatibility with legacy descriptor-based APIs.

1.title: Protobuf Descriptors for User–Vendor Account Management
1.summary: This cluster is generated Go protobuf code that exposes `Descriptor()` metadata for request/response messages used in user–vendor account workflows. It supports operations like bulk creation, upsert/update, detail lookup (by ID/vendor ID), pagination responses, vendor role/location payloads, and resend-vendor-email flows.

1.title: Loyalty Widget Protobuf Descriptors (Go)
1.summary: This cluster is generated Go protobuf runtime glue that exposes `Descriptor()` metadata for loyalty widget–related request/response and nested message types (create/edit, delete, find, content page, widget types, position/status updates). It enables protobuf reflection and serialization (and typically gRPC method plumbing) by providing access to the compiled `.proto` file descriptors and message type indices.

1.title: Supply Order Adjustment and Management
1.summary: This cluster handles supply order adjustments, inventory stock management, and related operations such as adjusting stock levels, creating supply orders, and managing inventory transitions.

1.title: Cart Service Implementation
1.summary: The cluster implements methods for retrieving and processing cart data, including calculating discounts and handling loyalty cart operations.

1.title: ERP Order Protobuf Message Descriptors
1.summary: This cluster consists of Go `Descriptor()` methods generated from `order/system/erp.proto` for ERP-related order request/response message types (order details, line items, delivery, payments, and external costs/campaigns). Each method returns the gzipped file descriptor bytes plus the message’s index path, enabling protobuf reflection, type registration, and runtime serialization support.

1.title: AB Testing Non-Login Experiment API
1.summary: This cluster provides methods for creating, finding, and managing non-login experiments and their variants, including attributes and responses.

1.title: GetListInboundCheckQty Validation & Error-Path Test Suite
1.summary: This cluster is a set of unit tests for the supply-order service method `GetListInboundCheckQty`, which lists inbound “check quantity” data for a Supply Order. It verifies gating rules (SO type/status, unloading/destination constraints, feature flags) and ensures correct behavior and error mapping when dependent repository/product/inventory/location lookups fail or when package/grouping data is missing or inconsistent.

1.title: Auth User-Role Protobuf Message Descriptors (Go)
1.summary: This cluster is Go code generated from `auth/userrole/userrole.proto`, providing `Descriptor()` methods for protobuf message types used to manage user-role assignments. It covers create/delete operations (single, bulk, by user), plus query responses for paginated role listings, user-role/location lookups, and resolving user IDs from role IDs and location context via protobuf reflection metadata.

1.title: User Vendor Role Protobuf Message Descriptors (Go)

It defines the schema metadata for creating and deleting user↔vendor role mappings (single and bulk), and for querying them (paginated, by user IDs, and location by user ID).

1.title: Product Search Reranking & Safety Post-Filtering
1.summary: This cluster reranks product search candidates using embedding similarity, then applies deterministic post-processing rules to remove or deprioritize results that are unsafe or contextually mismatched. It infers user intent signals (demographic needs like MPASI/pregnancy/diabetes/lansia, recipe context, single-item queries) and enforces exclusions for test/dummy items, bundles, and KI category mismatches, with brand-matching helpers and integration/unit tests validating the rule set.

1.title: WIMS Inventory & Stock Transfer Protobuf Message Descriptors
1.summary: This cluster is the Go protobuf-generated descriptor surface for WIMS inventory operations, covering inventory listing/detail models and request/response types. It defines messages for adjusting stock, transferring stock between warehouses (by SKU or asset), and validating/deriving eligibility for inventory transfers (including package ID checks).

1.title: Discovery Search Protobuf Descriptors for Pin Product & Recent Keyword APIs
1.summary: This cluster is generated Go protobuf code that defines message types and their `Descriptor()` metadata for Discovery/Search operations, primarily around “Pin Product” creation, updates, bulk status changes, listing/detail retrieval, type/filter-attribute lookup, and similar-item queries. It also includes request/response schemas for managing and retrieving recent keyword inventory data, enabling serialization and reflection for RPC and storage workflows.

1.title: WMS Purchase Request Protobuf Descriptor Cluster

It covers creating purchase requests (including auto-confirm), confirming details, fetching lists and detailed views, printing, deleting request details, querying supported request types, and updating expired purchase requests.

The tests cover paged `ListCampaigns`/`ListKeywords` behavior (cursor passthrough, sorting/filtering), ClickHouse-backed metric enrichment (e.g., ROAS/spent/items sold), budget status precedence, and graceful/propagated error handling when upstream systems fail or analytics rows are missing.

1.title: Vendor Page Protobuf Message Schema (Auth/Page)

It also models related entities (VendorPage, VendorElement, VendorEndpoint, VendorPagePermission) to support user-specific menu retrieval and permission pagination.

1.title: Protobuf Schema Descriptors for “Intools Page” API (Auth/Page)

It enables protobuf runtime features (type lookup, serialization support, introspection) for the Intools Page management API without containing the business logic itself.

1.title: Shipping Cost Promo Protobuf Descriptors

1.title: Disbursement Proto Message Descriptors (Go/Protobuf)

It enables protobuf runtime features—type identification, serialization support, and schema introspection—for gRPC/IPC communication in the disbursement domain.

1.title: Protobuf-to-JSON Schema Tool Input Generation
1.summary: This cluster converts protobuf message descriptors into JSON Schema objects suitable for exposing gRPC methods as “tools,” including correct handling of scalar types, enums, timestamps, nesting, maps/repeated fields, required-ness, and defaults, plus JSON serialization for debugging/tests. It also supports enriching the generated schema with external metadata (e.g., via CSV path resolution) and includes a prompt-block identifier (`IdentityChefBlock.Name`) used to label an identity-related block in the prompting system.

1.title: WIMS Protobuf Runtime Initialization
1.summary: This cluster is generated Go protobuf bootstrap code that initializes and registers WIMS `.proto` file descriptors, message types, and dependency ordering (e.g., calling `file_wims_common_proto_init()` first). It runs package `init()` hooks to configure reflection/exporters and make WIMS domain messages (areas, inventory, racks, picking/putaway, purchase/supply orders, replenishment, etc.) usable by the protobuf runtime for serialization and type metadata.

1.title: Loyalty Voucher Redemption Protobuf Message Descriptors
1.summary: This cluster is generated Go protobuf code providing `Descriptor()` metadata for loyalty voucher redemption-related request/response messages (find redeem vouchers, quotas, voucher loyalty details, redeem operations, and status updates tied to order create/cancel flows). It supports protobuf reflection/registration and backward compatibility, not the core redemption business logic itself.

1.title: Pricing Engine Price Change Protobuf Message Descriptors
1.summary: This cluster is the Go protobuf-generated `Descriptor()` implementations for pricing_engine “price change” request/response message types (list/get/download, approval detail retrieval, validation, submission, review, and update flows). It provides schema reflection metadata used by protobuf/runtime tooling for serialization, type identification, and backward-compatible API contract support around price-change workflows.

1.title: Referral Mission Protobuf API Messages (Go)

The `Descriptor()` methods provide protobuf reflection metadata for these message types to support serialization, type discovery, and runtime tooling.

Its role is to expose protobuf reflection metadata (raw file descriptors and type indices) used by serialization, compatibility checks, and tooling rather than implementing business logic.

It centralizes field-by-field mapping so upstream workflows can emit WIMS-compatible product registration requests consistently across all subcomponents.

The methods return the gzipped file descriptor bytes plus the message index, enabling protobuf reflection/registration and supporting serialization for payment order details such as line items, delivery, costs, modifiers, and references.

It provides the compiled schema metadata used by the Go protobuf runtime for reflection, type registration, and serialization across chat workflows like init, list conversations, get messages, send message, upload attachments, and submit CSAT.

1.title: WIMS Putaway Task Protobuf Descriptor Cluster
1.summary: This cluster is composed of Go protobuf-generated `Descriptor()` methods for WIMS putaway task request/response and entity message types (tasks, items, racks, activities, details). It provides runtime schema metadata used by protobuf reflection/serialization for operations like claiming/unassigning tasks, listing tasks/items (paginated), fetching task details, reserving/validating racks (including manual racks), and submitting task items.

1.title: Attendance Management System
1.summary: This cluster manages attendance records, approvals, and related operations for staff members.

1.title: Order System Protobuf Descriptor Stubs

Each `Descriptor()` returns the gzipped raw file descriptor (`file_order_system_proto_rawDescGZIP`) plus the message index, enabling protobuf reflection and type metadata at runtime.

1.title: Supply Order–Driven Manufacturing Auto-Conversion Workflow
1.summary: This cluster implements an automatic conversion pipeline that, upon Supply Order completion, derives conversion items and manufacturing rules, initializes a Manufacturing Order, calculates destination quantities, builds/saves order items, and applies stock adjustments with idempotency safeguards. It also coordinates event publishing (including alert variants) and robust failure handling via rollback classification (e.g., lock contention/business rejection) and stock adjustment reversals to keep inventory and order state consistent.

1.title: Redis Client Wrapper for Core Data Operations and TTL Management
1.summary: This cluster provides a thin Redis abstraction (likely over `redigo`) that wraps common commands for strings, counters, hashes, lists, sets, and sorted sets, returning typed results and handling connection lifecycle via a pool. It centralizes higher-level conveniences like byte-safe get/set, multi-key operations, scanning/keys, simple transactions (`MULTI`), and key expiration behaviors (`EXPIRE`, keep-TTL, set-if-not-exists).

1.title: OMS Integrator Protobuf Message Descriptors
1.summary: This cluster is generated Go protobuf code that exposes `Descriptor()` metadata for OMS integrator request/response message types covering order lifecycle operations (list, edit items/order, mark, accept/reject, cancel, and cancelability checks). It also includes message schemas for retrieving sales invoices from Accurate, with nested structures for invoice data, items, and pricing, plus the gzipped file descriptor used for protobuf reflection/registration.

It underpins request/response message types for listing and fetching thematics (by id/location), updating/toggling active state, deleting components, and scheduler-driven active-state responses, along with the gzipped raw file descriptor for reflection/registration.

The `Descriptor()` methods all reference the shared gzipped raw `.proto` descriptor (`file_pricing_engine_product_registration_product_registration_proto_rawDescGZIP`) to support protobuf reflection, dynamic type lookup, and compatibility with serialization/RPC tooling.

The `Descriptor()` methods and `file_fraud_whitelistdevice_proto_rawDescGZIP` provide the protobuf reflection descriptors and compressed schema needed for serialization, RPC wiring, and tooling interoperability.

1.title: User Intools Role Protobuf Descriptor Layer

The messages model CRUD and query operations for associating users with “Intools” roles, including bulk create/update/delete, lookups by user IDs, and paginated retrieval.
